appHero.title: Test @artifact/ui-lib components in a separate Vite app
appHero.description: Each section below targets a different part of the published library so visual tokens, interactivity, overlays, and controlled component behavior can be verified in one place.

navigationMove.label: Sidebar-controlled locale
navigationMove.description: Language switching now lives in the sidebar so the navigation shell tests localization, direction, and layout behavior together.

language.label: Language and locale
language.description: Powered by react-i18next so changing the language also updates direction and all static UI copy.

icons.title: Icon rendering test
icons.description: This section checks that the published icon package resolves correctly in the consumer app and that duotone styling still matches the surrounding design system.

home.label: Navigation home
home.description: A neutral icon for testing crisp edges, dual-color fills, and balanced sizing on light surfaces.

rotate.label: 3D rotate
rotate.description: Exercises the previously failing icon path and confirms the optimizer exclusion keeps module resolution stable.

alert.label: Alert state
alert.description: Useful for checking contrast and emphasis when the icon sits inside a stronger accent treatment.

icons.note: If these cards render with distinct duotone fills and no missing-module errors in development, the icon package is wired correctly.

buttons.title: Buttons and emphasis states
buttons.description: This validates color tokens, variants, loading states, and tooltip composition.
buttons.tooltip: Tooltip rendering proves floating styles and layering work in the consumer app.

checklist.title: Integration checklist
checklist.description: Use these checks while reviewing the demo sections.

items.buttons: Buttons should render distinct brand tones and variants, not browser defaults.
items.fields: Inputs, selects, sliders, and toggles should keep rounded token-driven styling and focus states.
items.table: The data table should support search, sorting, pagination, and row selection in the consumer app.
items.feedback: Dialog and toast feedback should prove overlay, portal, and animation styles are shipped correctly.
items.direction: Switching between English and Arabic should update layout direction for the full page and portal-based UI.
items.state: Controlled values should update live inside the consumer app without prop or state mismatches.

form.title: Form controls
form.description: Controlled inputs confirm that the packaged fields work correctly with local React state.
form.emailValid: Looks valid for this smoke test.
form.emailInvalid: Enter a valid email to test the error state.
form.qaNotesMessage: Text area styling should match the rest of the field system.

formBuilderExample.title: Form builder example
formBuilderExample.description: A dedicated example showing typed fields, built-in validation, conditional rendering, sticky submit actions, and a live preview panel.
formBuilderExample.note: Choose Platform or Operations to reveal an extra justification field. The form stays typed and validated through the builder config.

password.label: Password
password.helper: Use at least 8 characters and include one number.

accessReason.label: Access reason
accessReason.placeholder: Explain why elevated access is needed for this request
accessReason.helper: Shown only for Platform and Operations requests.

agree.label: I confirm this request follows the internal access policy

validation.passwordMin: Password must be at least 8 characters.
validation.passwordDigit: Password must include at least one number.
validation.accessReasonMin: Provide at least 20 characters for elevated access requests.
validation.agreeRequired: You must confirm the internal access policy.

actions.title: Ready to test form state
actions.description: Submit to trigger the builder validation flow or reset back to the initial values.

lastSubmission.title: Last submission
lastSubmission.empty: Submit the form once to capture a validated payload preview here.

selection.title: Toggles and selection controls
selection.description: This section stresses checkbox, switch, radio, and slider behavior with live state updates.

emailAlerts.label: Email alerts
emailAlerts.message: Tests checkbox label, hint text, and checked styling.

pushAlerts.label: Push alerts
pushAlerts.message: Uses the same control with a separate local state path.

canary.label: Canary
canary.message: Fastest iteration path for smoke testing.

selection.qualityMessage: Move the thumb to verify track, range, and value label rendering.

publishReady.label: Publish ready
publishReady.message: Switch styles should react cleanly across checked, unchecked, and focus states.

table.title: Data table example
table.description: This uses the packaged table component with client-side search, sorting, pagination, and row selection so you can verify layout behavior in both English and Arabic.
table.currentDirection: Current direction: {{direction}}. Switch the page language and confirm table alignment, search, pagination, and row selection still feel correct.
table.searchEmptyState: No results matched the current search.

feedback.title: Overlays and feedback
feedback.description: Dialog and toast are useful consumer-side checks because they depend on portals, layering, transitions, and global styling.
feedback.notice: Open the review dialog, then confirm it. You should see a styled modal, a dimmed overlay, and toast notifications that match the library design language.

toasts.reviewOpenedDescription: Overlay, portal, and toast layers are now active in the consumer app.
toasts.successDescription: The smoke test confirms feedback primitives respond correctly.
toasts.warningDescription: This verifies standalone toast calls outside dialog interactions.

dialog.title: External project dialog test
dialog.description: If this modal opens with a styled backdrop and surfaced panel, the published overlay components are working correctly in this consumer application.
dialog.body: This action simulates a QA sign-off flow using only the installed package components.

brand.title: Header and sidebar smoke test
brand.description: This shell verifies the packaged navigation components alongside the rest of the UI library.

header.title: Consumer app navigation
header.description: Header chips, trigger, live clock, and current date all come from the published package.

language.label: Language switcher
language.description: Switch the entire app language from here to verify RTL and LTR behavior inside the navigation shell.

chartsPage.title: Charts gallery
chartsPage.description: This page isolates chart rendering so different chart variants can be reviewed from the sidebar with their own layout, legends, and titles.

bar.title: Bar chart
bar.description: Useful for comparing discrete values across categories.

line.title: Line chart
line.description: Shows how a metric changes over time.

area.title: Area chart
area.description: Adds filled trend layers to compare volume over time.

pie.title: Pie chart
pie.description: Highlights proportion across a small set of categories.

doughnut.title: Doughnut chart
doughnut.description: A compact variation for status distribution and summaries.

radar.title: Radar chart
radar.description: Compares several dimensions against the same radial scale.

polarArea.title: Polar area chart
polarArea.description: Shows magnitude by segment with equal angles and radial depth.

gauge.title: Gauge chart
gauge.description: Shows progress toward a single target with a centered summary.

semiGauge.title: Semi-gauge chart
semiGauge.description: Uses a half arc to highlight completion in a compact dashboard view.

statisticsCard.title: StatisticsCard
statisticsCard.description: A compact KPI surface for key numbers, trend badges, and supporting context.

progress.title: Progress
progress.description: A ranked progress list for comparing milestones against a shared target.

revenue.title: Monthly revenue
revenue.description: Closed revenue across all active accounts.

conversion.title: Trial conversion
conversion.description: Share of trials upgraded in the last 30 days.

reliability.title: Service reliability
reliability.description: Measured uptime against the current availability target.

progress.title: Quarterly delivery
progress.description: Snapshot of team execution against the current quarter target.

otherTestsPage.title: Other tests
otherTestsPage.description: This page isolates breadcrumb and tabs so both components can be reviewed from the sidebar without mixing them into the home showcase sections.

breadcrumb.title: Breadcrumb navigation
breadcrumb.description: Use this example to verify separators, current-page styling, and router-backed links inside the consumer app.

checks.structureValue: Three breadcrumb levels with a current-page state.
checks.routingValue: The earlier items navigate with React Router links.

tabs.title: Tabs behavior
tabs.description: This sample checks tab switching, active-state visuals, and mixed trigger variants on one page.

overview.label: Overview
overview.title: Default tab state
overview.description: The first panel confirms the base tab style, spacing, and content container rendering.

focus.title: Focus and hover
focus.description: Move through the triggers with pointer and keyboard input to confirm visible state changes.

direction.title: Direction aware
direction.description: Switch the app language from the sidebar to verify the tab list still aligns and reads correctly in RTL.

notes.label: Notes
notes.title: What this page verifies
notes.description: Breadcrumb and tabs now have a dedicated route, so they can be tested independently from the home page sections.

status.title: Status tones
status.description: This section checks the new Status component across the main semantic tones and larger badge sizing.

notes.hierarchyDescription: The larger size should still feel compact while keeping icon and label spacing balanced.
notes.toneDescription: Each tone should remain distinct on light surfaces without drifting into button-like emphasis.

skeleton.title: Skeleton loading states
skeleton.description: This layout mixes text, block, and circle skeletons so spacing, radius, and animation can be reviewed together.
skeleton.note: The final block is intentionally static to confirm the non-animated state still matches the same visual system.

tooltip.title: Updated tooltip design
tooltip.description: These samples verify the refreshed tooltip surface, motion, arrow handling, and notch behavior in one place.

default.title: Default notch
default.description: Tests the default tooltip styling with the new floating surface and built-in notch.
default.content: Default tooltip with the updated compact dark treatment.

arrow.title: Arrow mode
arrow.description: Uses the explicit arrow variant to confirm the alternative pointer style still aligns correctly.

rich.title: Rich content
rich.description: Exercises multi-line content and a different placement so the layout stays readable.
rich.contentBody: Latency is within target, but one region is still warming caches after rollout.

accordion.title: Accordion variants
accordion.description: This section checks both contained and separated accordion treatments, plus disclosure and keyboard behavior.

contained.title: Contained layout
contained.description: Verifies the grouped accordion surface with shared border, spacing, and open-state rhythm.

keyboard.title: Keyboard navigation
keyboard.description: Use arrow keys, Home, and End to confirm focus movement between triggers remains predictable.

separated.title: Separated cards
separated.description: Checks the alternate card-like variant where each item has its own surface and shadow.

states.title: Disclosure states
states.description: Open multiple items here to verify trigger rotation, content spacing, and transition timing.

carousel.title: Carousel layout
carousel.description: This sample checks slide spacing, button positioning, and horizontal scrolling behavior in the updated carousel.
carousel.note: Use the arrow buttons and keyboard navigation to confirm the carousel responds cleanly without layout jumps.

1.title: Overview card
1.description: Confirms the first slide aligns correctly inside the viewport and leaves room for navigation controls.

2.title: Spacing check
2.description: Makes the inter-slide gutter easy to inspect across compact and wider viewport widths.

3.title: Navigation check
3.description: Ensures the final slide can still be reached smoothly and the next button disables at the end.
